title: Three-bean salad – braggy
excerpt: Three-bean salad is a lifesaver in my fridge. It keeps for ages and fills you up. It's such a rewarding, colourful salad. There's nothing that needs to be cooked. Just stir it together and enjoy. three tins of beans of your choice 1 chilli (optional) 1 green pepper 3 cloves garlic 2 onions …
html:
Three-bean salad is a lifesaver in my fridge. It keeps for ages and fills you up. It's such a rewarding, colourful salad. There's nothing that needs to be cooked. Just stir it together and enjoy.

  • three tins of beans of your choice
  • 1 chilli (optional)
  • 1 green pepper
  • 3 cloves garlic
  • 2 onions
  • 5ml cumin (1 teaspoon)
  • 5 ml coriander (1 teaspoon)
  • 7,5 ml salt (1 1/2 teaspoon)
  • 5 ml ground pepper (1 teaspoon)
  • 200ml brown vinegar
  • 200 ml oil
  • 80 ml brown sugar

If you don't have cumin or coriander, you can leave it out. The same goes for the chilli if you're not one for a bit of heat. Taste the sauce to see how sweet you'd like it. Sweeter, just add a bit more sugar.

 

NOW:

  1. drain the beans and tip them into your salad bowl
  2. chop the chilli, garlic, green pepper and onions finely and mix it with your beans
  3. now pour all the rest of the ingredients into a bowl and stir until the sugar has dissolved
  4. stir your sauce into your bean mixture
  5. let your bean salad stand in the fridge for at least half a day to “develop”.

The longer the bean salad stands, the better it gets. I added chickpeas to my beans. If you use beans in tomato sauce, don't throw the tomato sauce away, mix it into your vinegar mixture. Enjoy and let it rip.
